observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
prepare:
a. Adjusting entries to recognize stale checks and bank credit memos as
a debit to the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account and a
credit to Prior Years’ Adjustment (for prior years’ check) and a credit
to Government Equity (for current years’ check issued).
b. Adjusting entries recognizing the bank debit memos as a credit to the
Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account and a debit to Prior
Years’ Adjustment (for prior year accounts) and a debit to Government
Equity account (for CY 2024 bank debit memos).

Management Comment
1.9 The Municipal Accountant-Designate prepared the necessary adjusting entries
to account for stale checks, bank credit memos and bank debit memos, and
subsequently provided the Audit Team with copies of the corresponding General
Journal entries dated January 31, 2025.

2.28 Due to the deficiencies noted, we recommended and Management agreed
that:
a. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer submit the certified list of tax due and
collectible for a given year to support the set-up of RPT Receivable and
SET Receivable in January of every year by the Municipal Accountant
– designate;
b. The Municipal Accountant – designate prepare journal entries to
present fairly the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable and its
corresponding Deferred RPT and Deferred SET as of December 31,
2024, to wit:
b.1 RPT Receivable ₱1,486,465.92
Deferred RPT ₱1,486,465.92
To set up RPT Receivable as of December 31, 2024
b.2 SET Receivable ₱1,486,465.92
Deferred SET ₱1,486,465.92
To set up SET Receivable as of December 31, 2024
b.3 Government Equity ₱265,640.81
RPT - Basic ₱265,640.81
To recognize the Municipality’s share on the Advance Receipts for
RPT – Basic collected in CY 2024 that caused overstatement of tax
revenue in CY 2024
b.4 Government Equity ₱332,051.02
SET Income ₱332,051.02
To recognize the Municipality’s share on the Advance Receipts for
SET Income collected in CY 2024 that caused overstatement of SET
Revenue in CY 2024
b.5 Discount on RPT – Basic ₱61,428.86
Government Equity ₱61,428.86
To recognize Municipality’s share on the discount from Advance
Receipts recorded in CY 2024 that caused understatement of RPT –
Basic in CY 2024
b.6 Discount on Special Education Tax ₱76,786.08
Government Equity ₱76,786.08
To recognize Municipality’s share on the discount from Advance
Receipts recorded in CY 2024 that caused understatement of SET
Income in CY 2024
c. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the monthly Abstract
of RPT Collections showing the collections from advance receipts,
current year, delinquent accounts, discount given from advance
receipts and current year accounts, and fines and penalties from
delinquent current and previous years’ accounts to facilitate the
recording to appropriate accounts by the Municipal Accountant –
designate;
d. The Municipal Accountant – designate strictly observe the prescribed
journal entries for the collection of property taxes based on the monthly
Abstract of RPT Collections submitted by the OIC Municipal Treasurer
under the General Fund and Special Education Fund books; and
e. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant – designate
present at gross amounts the actual collections of property taxes to its
appropriate accounts and avoid offsetting of discounts and
aggregating fines and penalties to the RPT – Basic and SET Income.

Management Comment
2.29 The Municipal Accountant-Designate prepared the adjusting entries for RPT
and SET Receivables, including the related Deferred RPT and Deferred SET
accounts, and provided the Audit Team with copies of the General Journal Nos.
100-2025-01-02 and 200-2025-01-01, both dated Januarys 31, 2025.
2.30 Furthermore, during the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer stated that she
had already commenced the submission of the monthly Abstract of RPT
Collections to the Municipal Accountant.

Management Comment
3.1 The Municipal Accountant-Designate provided a copy of General Journal No.
100-2025-01-01 dated January 31, 2025, which reclassified liabilities for
salaries, benefits and other emoluments from the Accounts Payable account to
the Due to Officers and Employees account.

4.9 We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal
Accountant:
a. Reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts
and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances,
including recognizing all outstanding reciprocal entries and ensuring
the amounts in both accounts offset each other properly;
b. Maintain subsidiary ledgers with detailed description to identify the
nature of each transactions in the reciprocal accounts; and
c. Conduct periodic reconciliations to ensure the accuracy of the records
and the fair presentation of the financial statements.

Management Comment
4.10 The Municipal Accountant-Designate provided a copy of General Journal No.
100-2025-03-01 date March 31, 2025, recognizing the outstanding reciprocal
entries in the General Fund books.

Management Comment
5.21 The designated GSO and Municipal Accountant averred that they have already
started the preparation of the Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards,
respectively. Further, the concerned personnel from the MDRRMO and MHO
committed to submitting the distribution list or dispense list by April 30, 2025.

Management Comment
6.18 During the exit conference, the GSO-Designate stated that the preparation of
property cards for PPE items had already started.

7.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE create a Disposal
Committee following the establishment of the Inventory Committee by
April 2025, which will undertake the necessary action/s for the application
and immediate disposal of all the unserviceable properties to avoid further
deterioration and losses due to theft.

8.11 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Engineer prioritize the preparation and submission of
detailed plans and program of works for all development projects,
including clear timelines and project specifications, to facilitate the
timely and efficient implementation of projects;
b. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), in
coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, closely monitor the
implementation of planned development projects funded by the 20
percent Development Fund and to adopt methods to ensure the timely
conduct of the procurement process, in order to optimally utilize the
fund; and
c. The Municipal Engineer and MPDC ensure the immediate
commencement of any development projects once funds are realigned
and all necessary plans are in place to ensure that the intended socio-
economic and environmental benefits are achieved within the planned
timeframe.

9.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the GAD Focal Point
System shall:
a. Prepare a GAD Agenda by August 2025, in consultation with local
functionaries and stakeholders at the beginning of the Local Chief
Executive’s term of leadership, to serve as the basis for the
preparation of the annual GAD Plan and Budget.
b. Conduct a Gender Analysis/Assessment during the formulation of
programs, projects and activities, and establish and maintain a sex-
disaggregated database/information system to identify gender issues
and gaps within the Municipality that need to be addressed.

11.12 We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the
provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement, and that the Technical
Working Group shall convene within two months after the exit conference
to develop the necessary action plan for the effective implementation and
sustainability of the Rice Production: Marketing Support (RPMS) Project.

12.10 We recommended and Management agreed to implement preventive
measures to avoid the recurrence of the identified issues and ensure that
all future time-sensitive procurements are conducted in full compliance
with Republic Act No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations. Additionally, the Bids and Awards Committee shall strictly
adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR in all
procurement activities conducted by the Municipality.

13.14 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Ensure strict compliance with the use of official receipts or invoices
for all claims, particularly those exceeding the threshold set in COA
Circular 2021-001;
b. Stop the practice of making cash payments for the purchase of goods
and services, except for petty expenses and those incurred in
emergency situations. Instead, adhere to the procedures for Small
Value Procurement as provided under the Revised IRR of RA No.
9184, and issue checks payable to the supplier/creditor in accordance
with Section 93 of PD No. 1445; and
c. Ensure the completeness and propriety of all required supporting
documents for disbursements, in accordance with applicable laws,
rules and regulations, to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

14.12 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Submit an approved LGU Capacity Development Agenda to prove that
the study tour activity is an integral part of the LGU’s CapDev program,
as required under DILG – DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No.
2 dated September 23, 2016;
b. Secure ex-post facto authorization for the study tour from DILG or its
appropriate bureau/office/unit by May 2025;
c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG – DBM JMC No. 2 in the
conduct of study tours.

We recommended and the OIC-

for the reclassification of completed

PPE accounts; and

depreciation expenses for the

Notes to Financial Statements.

consideration and approval.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Prepare the necessary adjustments for the reclassification of
completed projects from CIP to its appropriate PPE accounts; and
b. Compute the allocation for depreciation expenses for the completed
projects and recognize the amount of the Accumulated Depreciation
and Prior Year’s Adjustment as a result of the reclassification.

2.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the General Services Officer to: (a) ensure that the tangible
items below ₱50,000.00 previously issued to end-users and classified
as PPE are covered by Property Acknowledgment Receipts otherwise,
prepare and issue the necessary Inventory Custodian Slip for items,
and (b) prepare and maintain the forms, registry and reports for
purposes of record-keeping over semi-expendable properties as
prescribed in Section 4.7 of COA Circular No. 2022-004; and
b. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: (a) make the necessary
adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of affected
accounts, (b) update existing PPE and Inventory records and prepare
and maintain the Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Card, and (c)
ensure that the necessary disclosures required under Item 33 of IPSAS
3 in the implementation of the change in accounting policy are
presented in the Municipality’s Notes to Financial Statements.

3.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to recognize the procurement of
palay to the appropriate Inventory account using the perpetual
inventory method in the accounting of inventories, and prepare and
maintain the required Supplies Ledger Card; and
b. Instruct the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the
required Stock Card and initiate the conduct of a physical count of
palay stocks to determine the necessary adjustments to the books and
inventory records.

4.14 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger
Cards for inventory assets;
b. General Services Officer to prepare the SSMI for all RIS approved and
issued and submit it to the OIC-Municipal Accountant;
c. MDRRMO, MSWDO, and MHO to submit the distribution list for all
supplies distributed as of December 31, 2023; and
d. Inventory Committee to conduct periodic inventory-taking to establish
the existence of inventories reported in the books.

5.13 We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office on the determination
of the implemented and completed projects of the Municipality and
retrieve the appropriate and necessary project documents for the
preparation of journal entry vouchers to recognize completed projects
in its appropriate PPE accounts; and
b. Prepare the CIP Ledger Cards for every implemented project to
monitor progress and the eventual transfer to its appropriate PPE
accounts.

6.12 We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Submit the lacking documents required under Sections 6.1.7 and
6.1.8.3 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated
January 8, 2015; and
b. Submit the cash advances vouchers for Confidential Fund within the
prescribed period, and ensure the completeness of the supporting
documents pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the aforementioned
Joint Circular.

7.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to request for an extension
to utilize the unobligated amount of the grant beyond the validity period
of December 31, 2021 to establish the propriety and validity of the charges
against the said fund in CY 2022 in compliance with Section 4 (1) of PD
No. 1445; otherwise, the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer return the unobligated funds as of December 31, 2021 to the
National Treasury/Office of the President, in accordance with the
provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by the
Municipality and Office of the President.

8.12 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) retrieve the required reports
pertaining to the utilization of the unexpended balances of the LDRRM
from 2017 to 2022, such as the approved LDRRMC resolutions and
SAAOB with the coordination of the Municipal DRRM Officer, Municipal
Budget Officer, and Sangguniang Bayan, (b) reconcile the balances of
the unexpended amount of LDRRM from previous years presented in
the Notes to the Financial Statements, and (c) prepare subsidiary
ledgers of the unexpended balances of LDRRM each year from CY 2017
to 2022; and
b. Direct the Municipal DRRM Officer to submit the monthly Report of
Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund within the prescribed period.

9.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Consider the provision of additional manpower to the accounting
office or the hiring of a Certified Public Accountant to head the
municipal accounting office and the availment of the eNGAS and/or
eBudget from the COA to facilitate the timely and systematic
generation of financial reports; and
b. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant hasten the preparation and
submission of the financial reports and adhere to the prescribed
period for submission to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies
noted, if any.

10.13 We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Issue written notices/demand letters to the concerned officials and
employees of the Municipality with outstanding cash advances for the
immediate liquidation thereof;
b. Stop the granting of additional cash advances to those officials and
employees with unliquidated and outstanding cash advances until the
full liquidation of their previous cash advances; and
c. Initiate the preparation of the necessary documents pertaining to
dormant cash advances and file a request of authority to write off to
the Commission on Audit for consideration and approval.

11.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Assessor to fast-track the conduct of physical surveys to
check and determine whether all real properties within the locality are
properly listed in the Assessment Rolls; prepare and maintain separate
Assessment Rolls for taxable real properties and for exempt properties
as prescribed in the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and
Assessment Operations; and furnish the Municipal Treasurer copies
of the updated Assessment Rolls for taxable and exempt properties;
and
b. Municipal Treasurer to reconcile and update the Real Property Tax
Account Register (RPTAR)/Real Property Tax Index Card (RPTIC) with
the updated Assessment Roll from the Municipal Assessor and furnish
the OIC-Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list showing the
name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.

12.13 We recommended and the Management agreed to submit the
aforementioned lacking supporting documents for the reimbursement of
foreign travel expenses and the Sanggunian Bayan submit clarification of
the specific purpose of the appropriated Traveling Expenses in the two
approved supplemental budgets, whether it was for local or foreign travel.

13.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee to submit justification for awarding the
procurement at hand despite the absence of the specific authority on
the persons attending and signing bid documents and the Notice of
Awards, and diligently examine the authority and validity of the
suppliers to ensure that the requirements for the procurement of
goods, infrastructure projects and consulting services are complied
with in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;
b. Municipal Budget Officer or the end-user to submit an explanation on
the reason that the Purchase Order and payment were entered into and
made to the person with no specific authority from the registered owner
of the winning bidder; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents for the
awarded procurement, as listed above.

14.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Submit justification to prove reasonableness for the procurement of
the afore-cited items above the DPRI of the DOH; and
b. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Health Office
to procure drugs and medicines at reasonable prices guided by the
applicable DPRI set by DOH in accordance with DOH Administrative
Order No. 2019-0040 dated October 14, 2019, to ensure procurement
that is most advantageous to the government.

15.9 We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the
lacking documentary requirement as enumerated above and ensure that
future procurement of land/real property be duly supported with complete
documentary requirements as provided under Section 14.1 of COA
Circular No. 2023-004.

We recommended and the Management
agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant derecognize
those PPE items costing below the
capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00
from the PPELCs of the affected PPE
accounts;
b. The GSO-Designate prepare the
Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) for
the disposal of unserviceable/damaged
properties; and
c. An adequate number of personnel shall
be assigned and the coordination of the
department heads shall be secured in
order to complete the conduct of One-
Time Cleansing of the PPE accounts,
and to reconcile the RPCPPE and the
PPELC to provide accurate reporting of
PPE in the Statement of Financial
Position.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Chairperson of the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with the posting requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 to promote accountability and transparency in the conduct of Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary journal entry for the unrecorded fund transfers, and ensure that book reconciling items are appropriately recorded to reflect the correct and accurate balance of the Cash in Bank -Local Currency Current Account and other related accounts.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to return the original Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents, for those checks which have been unclaimed, to the Municipal Accountant who shall prepare a Journal Voucher to record the transactions as Accounts Payable;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to these payees of the existence of the check at least one month before a check becomes stale; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to strictly observe Section 104 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume 1 in handling spoiled and stale checks.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Agriculturist to submit inventory report of the biological assets, its location, the quantity (if any), the list of distribution to whom it was released and the Memorandum of Understanding, if any, between the municipality and the beneficiary. Submit to the Municipal Accountant the said reports for reconciliation with the accounting records; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile its accounting records with the Municipal Agriculturist. Then, draw journal voucher for any adjustments to the Biological Asset accounts.

We recommended that the Management:
Create an Inventory Committee with adequate number of members to complete the conduct of physical inventory within the prescribed timeframe, who will coordinate with the General Services Office in the preparation of the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for every semester of a given year, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002. Likewise, the GSO will coordinate with the Accounting Office for the periodic reconciliation of both records, as prescribed in the said COA Circular; and
Require the Supply Unit to strictly observe the preparation and weekly submission of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Accounting Unit for appropriate recording of the supplies and materials issuances.

We recommended that the Management:
Issue written notices/demand letters to the concerned accountable officers requiring the immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances;
Prepare request of write-off to those dormant accounts together with the necessary documentation particularly to those accountable officers who are already deceased in accordance to COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
Require the submission of liquidation reports for previously granted cash advances before processing a new one pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit a justification why the realignment or augmentation of appropriations were not covered by an appropriation ordinance;
Require the Sanggunian Bayan to pass an appropriation ordinance to give the LCE an absolute authority for the realignment and/or augmentation of any items in the Annual Budget; and
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that no disbursements will be made from realignment and/or augmented funds unless supported with an appropriation ordinance.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to accomplish the necessary Status of Work Accomplished and As-Built Plans for every infrastructure project implemented By Administration which will be validated by the Municipality’s Inspectorate Committee, and will be the basis for the issuance of the related Certificate of Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all progress billings pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and such other applicable laws, rules and regulations; and
Submit the completed contracts and/or purchase orders within the prescribed period to conduct necessary auditorial review.

We recommended that the Management:
Ensure that Municipal Development Council meticulously plan the programs and projects to be funded under the 20% Development Fund, proper scheduling for its implementation, and determine the readiness of its procurement requirements, including the projects under the Continuing Appropriations;
Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Program of Works based on the available appropriation of the certain projects, programs, or activities; and
The Management to determine and ensure the projects, programs, or activities to be funded out from the DSWD before providing for an appropriation to avoid non-utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Management properly implement the LDRRMIP and optimize the utilization of its LDRRM Fund to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster risks, including projected climate risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Community.

We recommended that the Management clearly identify the P/P/A in the LDRRMF Investment Plan so as not to confuse its purpose, and utilize funds for the specific purpose it was created, in consonance with Section 336 of Republic Act No. 7160.
We recommended that the Management refrain from allocating the DRRM Fund on PPAs that do not directly address calamity-related risks and scenarios.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly observe the provisions of the COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 particularly on the supporting documents and the timeline for the submission of the grant of cash advances for Confidential Fund and its liquidation. On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant is encouraged to familiarize himself with the provisions of the JC. Finally, submit the required documents to support the DV for all CAs of CF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through his liaison officer submit the transmittal letter duly received by the ICFAU for the liquidation of his cash advance for Confidential Expenses to its Municipal Accountant. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to restore the amount liquidated to Advances to Special Disbursing Officer. Finally, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant to observe the provisions for grant and liquidation of cash advance for Confidential Fund stated in COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the submission of any of the necessary documents provided under item 6.2 Annex E of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to support the withdrawal of retention money for any completed projects to protect the Municipality to any contingencies that may arise from uncorrected defects and/or third-party liabilities.

Recommendation:

Direct the Accountant to:

Analyze the effects of each transaction and recognize the affected account classification;
Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the said errors; and
Ensure the appropriate use of accounts in recording the Municipality’s financial transactions.
observations.managementAction: Prepared adjusting journal entries
Submitted lacking documents

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

Stop immediately the practice of paying checks as reimbursements of expenditures paid by officials and/or employees, and issue check directly payable to the supplier/creditor pursuant to Section 93 of PD No. 1445;

Ensure strict compliance with the relevant laws, rules and regulations on procurement; and

Ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all disbursements in accordance with applicable laws, rules and regulations.

Recommendations:

Require the Accounting Office and Property Unit to strictly adhere to Section 258 of the GAM for LGUs on the maintenance of PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, and ensure timely reconciliation of these records to facilitate verification and adjustment of any discrepancies noted. 

Instruct the Inventory Committee to follow the format provided in Appendices 62 to 66 of the said GAM in the preparation of RCPPE to ensure inclusion of all necessary and relevant information therein, and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation. 

Require the Property/Supply Unit to render a report of all defective or non-functional PPE items for proper recording and disposal using the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

Recommendations:

Ensure that the effects of the transactions, specifically pertaining to Biological Assets, are faithfully represented in the financial statements by recognizing the additions, reductions, gains and losses through sales and changes in fair values thereof.

Direct the Accounting Office and GSO to maintain ledger cards and property cards for biological assets.

Require periodic physical inventory of biological assets owned by the Municipality at least once a year, and reconciliation of accounting and property/supply records at every reporting period.

Recommendations:

Comply strictly with the guidelines that only allowable projects enumerated under Section 3 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017, shall be charged against 20% Development Fund; and

Ensure that all developmental projects to be funded under the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures as prescribed in aforementioned Joint Memorandum Circular.

Recommendations:

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to return the original Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents for those checks which have not been claimed to the Municipal Accountant who shall prepare a Journal Voucher to record the transactions as Accounts Payable; 

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check at least one month before a check becomes stale; and

Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to strictly observe Section 104, Chapter 3 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume 1 in handling spoiled and stale checks.

Recommendations:

Ensure strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue final demand letters to the accountable officers who are no longer connected to the Municipality, requiring them to liquidate all long outstanding advances; and

File the appropriate request for authority to write-off unliquidated cash advances of those deceased accountable officers in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendations:

Ensure strict adherence to the relevant provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs-Department of Defense Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015, in the granting of cash advances for Confidential Fund and the submission of the related DV; and

Submit the required documents, as enumerated in Sections 6.1.7 and 6.1.8.3 of the same Joint Circular, to support the DV for the CA with check nos. 905686 and 905458.

Recommendations:

Submit the required documentary evidence to support the existence of extension of work/extra work rendered, the authority or legal basis for its rendition, the program of work and its timeline, the quantified physical accomplishments, and other pertinent supporting documents to the claim; and

Ensure that transactions are duly supported with the complete and appropriate supporting documents before approving the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Submitted lacking documents

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

Submit the Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption for January to December 2020; and

Comply strictly with the submission of the said reports and ensure that the Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished and duly approved, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61.

Recommendation:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the excess contract cost of ₱179,449.95 from the claims of Adse Enterprises for the upgrading of Tulips Street. Henceforth, the LGU through its Municipal Engineer shall adhere to the provisions of DPWH-DUPA in the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract.
observations.managementAction: Submitted a variation order

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit reason/justification why the procuring entity did not use the prices in the Construction Material Prices Data (CMPD) for the prices of construction materials in the preparation of Program of Works/ ABC or the project – Construction of Reinforced Concrete Deck Girder (RCDG) Bridge amounting ₱7,258,416.65.
observations.managementAction: Submitted a justification

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all related disbursements are supported with complete documentary requirements as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

Recommendations:

Submit justification/s regarding the existence of personnel with delinquent loans; and

Require the monthly deduction from the monthly compensation of employees the loan amortizations due to GSIS and remit the same in accordance with Sections 14.2 and 14.3 of RA No. 8291 to avoid the occurrence of delinquent loans from members.
